behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Escalation over Unit passes THREE-GATE: evidence: "request sent to the external API" and "complete list of valid taxonomy labels"; miss at lower level: a unit test of payload builder can pass while classifier->client handoff drops/overwrites labels; boundary type: handoff-state. Integration-BRAKE: no real OS/network requirement, no model-output correctness, no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake in-process API client that records outbound payload. Invoke classify() with a taxonomy and assert recorded request contains the full taxonomy set (no missing labels) at dispatch time.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Escalation over Unit passes THREE-GATE: evidence: "When the API returns... module returns 'unknown'"; miss at lower level: pure normalization unit test can pass while integration path bypasses normalization and forwards raw provider label; boundary type: handoff-state. Integration-BRAKE checks: no real infra required, no dependence on model quality, no multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process fake API response (e.g., "medical") not present in taxonomy. Call module entrypoint and assert returned value is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text scopes to "normalization step" with "identical raw API responses"; failure boundary is local deterministic transformation logic. Higher levels rejected: no required module handoff evidence, no real infra, no real model-output dependency, no multi-step chain.
behaviors.test_description: Directly test normalization function/object with repeated identical raw inputs and assert identical normalized output each run (including casing/whitespace variants if applicable).

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Escalation over System passes THREE-GATE: evidence: "repeated live API calls" and consistency tied to "provider response"; miss at lower level: mocked/stubbed calls always return fixed labels and cannot expose real model/provider variability; boundary type: model-output. Rejected Workflow because behavior is a single classification decision per call, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real provider calls for identical document+taxonomy under controlled settings, capture raw provider outputs and final labels, and assert label stability when raw provider outputs are unchanged; flag drift when outputs vary.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Escalation over Unit passes THREE-GATE: evidence: "before dispatch" and policy applied during "request construction"; miss at lower level: token-budget helper may pass unit tests while assembled request still dispatches over-budget due to wiring/order bug; boundary type: handoff-state. Higher levels rejected: no required real infra, no model-output dependence, no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small budget, call module with over-budget input, and use a fake API client to assert either truncated payload or blocked dispatch per policy, and never an unmodified over-budget send.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Escalation over System passes THREE-GATE: evidence: "Provider-reported token usage" requires a real AI provider response; miss at lower level: local fakes cannot validate true provider-side token accounting and can mask overruns that occur only with real model calls; boundary type: model-output (real AI API execution path). Rejected Workflow: single-call budget check, not multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls on representative documents and assert provider-reported token usage metadata is <= configured budget for each call; record failures with request payload and provider usage fields.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Escalation over Unit passes THREE-GATE: evidence: "API signals... waits and retries" describes interaction between retry policy and API client; miss at lower level: unit-testing backoff math alone can pass while orchestration still returns error without retry; boundary type: handoff-state. Integration-BRAKE: can be reproduced with in-process stub responses/timer control; no real model-output correctness; no multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client that returns rate-limit error then success, with controllable clock/sleeper. Assert module performs wait/backoff, retries, and returns successful label instead of propagating initial error.
